incidents.canonical_name: Earthquake 27 km N of Caraballeda June 2026
incidents.summary: Two powerful earthquakes, including a magnitude 7.2 event, struck west of Caracas, Venezuela, on June 24, 2026, resulting in 1,943 deaths and damaging nearly 59,000 buildings. International rescue teams, including units from Costa Rica and South Florida, assisted in the aftermath, notably rescuing a man trapped under rubble for eight days.

incidents.canonical_name: Earthquake 107 km SSW of Sarangani June 2026
incidents.summary: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck off Maasim, Sarangani, Philippines, on June 8, 2026, resulting in 81 deaths, 31 missing persons, and over 87,000 damaged houses. Infrastructure losses are estimated at approximately PHP 1.47 billion, prompting the National Housing Authority and DSWD to distribute millions in financial aid to affected families. Subsequent earthquakes, including one with a magnitude of 6.7, were also reported in the region later in June.

incidents.canonical_name: Ebola outbreak DR Congo June 2026
incidents.summary: The WHO has declared a public health emergency of international concern following an outbreak of Ebola disease caused by the Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. As of May 15, 2026, the DRC reported 246 suspected cases and 80 deaths, while Uganda confirmed two imported cases. Response efforts are focused on surveillance, infection prevention, and cross-border coordination, though efforts in the DRC's Ituri province are hindered by ongoing conflict and humanitarian crises.

incidents.canonical_name: Nipah outbreak India June 2026
incidents.summary: India notified the WHO of two laboratory-confirmed cases of Nipah virus infection in West Bengal involving healthcare workers at a private hospital in Barasat. Health authorities tested over 190 contacts, all of whom were negative, and have implemented enhanced surveillance and infection control measures. WHO assesses the public health risk as moderate at the sub-national level and low at the national, regional, and global levels.
